pages.full_text:
N.E section through [?Palud] & parked at Couloir Sampson (columns of the temple) & walked to see Baume (cave) of Pigeons through dark tunnels, originally cut [?for] for water canals. In one part the ceiling had fallen in. We saw an ugly toad. There were people in crash helmets & wet [?suits] jumping off rocks. Would-be pot holers. Also rock climbers & people walking. When the sun came up & hit us, we turned back.
	Back in the car we went over Pont de l'Artuby, the highest bungee jump in Europe (fortunately not working) We stopped at le [?Mesclun] where a tributary joins.
	Our trip took us [arrow sketch] like this around the Canyon [?Rim].
	It was difficult to find a place for lunch because so many people had parked in shady spots by the road.
	We looked at several belvederes & got home just after they closed the shower house & washing machines. So I read in the shade for 40 minutes & then did 2 loads including the bedding. While it dried, John beat me again
pages.route_sketch_description: Small inline arrow sketch illustrating the loop route taken around the canyon — a curved arrow shape drawn mid-sentence to show the shape of the trip around the Canyon.
pages.notes: Page number not visible in top-left corner — may be on a preceding page or cut off by scan. 'Palud' likely refers to La Palud-sur-Verdon, a village at the start of the Route des Crêtes in the Gorges du Verdon area. '[?Mesclun]' could be 'Mescla' (the confluence point where the Verdon joins the Artuby tributary is known locally). 'Canyon [?Rim]' — last word hard to read, could be 'Rim' or 'Run' or 'Rim'. Wet [?suits] — partially obscured word. The text continues onto the right page ('again / at Scrabble.').

pages.full_text:
at Scrabble.
	At supper, we discussed how many [?kito] Kilo joules are in Yop — [strikethrough word] about 2400k which means Charles could walk up 350m on the energy – 1 kilo can be carried up 1 metre on 10 [?joules].
pages.notes: Page contains a large glued-in postcard/photograph of the Gorges du Verdon canyon with a river at the bottom; printed caption reads 'le torrent' on the left and 'Gorges du Verdon' on the right in large type. Handwritten page number 13 appears at top centre. 'Yop' appears to be a brand name (French yogurt drink). The struck-through word before 'about 2400k' looks like '30' or a similar number. '[?kito]' appears before 'Kilo joules' — possibly a false start/correction. '[?joules]' — last word partially cut off at page bottom.
